title: Syracuse Mayor Walsh ready to accept some budget cuts; council leader not impressed
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh signaled willingness to accept $2.5M+ in budget cuts to eliminate a proposed 2% tax increase, but Syracuse Common Council leadership (Councilor Pat Hogan) rejected compromise and indicated they will override mayoral objections to their $16M reduction from the $348.4M budget proposal. The impasse centers on reserve fund usage ($27M proposed vs. council preference) and service impacts, with the council's final decision expected May 27.

political_signal.description: Councilor Hogan (mayoral candidate) rejected Walsh's compromise overture and indicated predetermined council override strategy; characterized mayor's budget defense as 'political theater' and 'scare tactics,' signaling hardened partisan positioning ahead of municipal election.

processing_notes: Entity extraction note: 'Syracuse University' listed in mechanical extractions but does not appear in article text; removed from entity_refs.
Dollar amounts validated: $2.5M (cuts accepted), $348.4M (mayor's budget), $16M (council reduction), $60 (tax impact example), $27M (reserves proposed), $120M (reserve fund balance).
Critical date: May 27 council meeting for objection review; May 16 (Monday) mayoral objection deadline.
Service impact specifics: police downtown security details and Armory Square nighttime patrols eliminated under council plan.
Political context: Hogan's mayoral candidacy noted as potential bias factor in opposition stance.
